## Preceding prose

> *Example:* The following should be typeset as a single staff in the printed score.
> *Alternative usage example:* The above could also be achieved using [inline fields](#inline_field_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), the `I:` form instead of `%%` and a `r:remark` in place of the [comment](#comment_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), i.e.
> Finally, note that if the the `` character is not being used to indicate [score line-breaks](#score_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), then the backslash is effectively redundant.
> *Recommendation to users:* If you find that you are using backslash symbols on most lines of [music code](#music_code_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), then consider instead using "`I:linebreak `" or "`I:linebreak $`" which will mean that all the [code line-breaks](#code_line-break_definition "abc:
> `y` can be used to add extra space between the surrounding notes; moreover, [chord symbols](#chord_symbols "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") and [decorations](#decorations "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") can be attached to it, to separate them from notes.
